Testimonial for Mr. Rallo – Trusted Legal Advocate

Our family has had the privilege of working with Mr. Rallo and his firm on various legal matters in Lake County, Illinois, dating back to the early 1980s. Over the decades, he has consistently been our go-to legal expert whenever we’ve needed guidance or representation.

Mr. Rallo is not only deeply knowledgeable about the law, but he is also a tenacious advocate who truly fights for his clients. His dedication, integrity, and thorough understanding of our legal rights have made a meaningful difference in our lives.

If you take the time to get to know Mr. Rallo as we have, you’ll quickly see why we trust him implicitly. He brings both experience and passion to every case, and we wholeheartedly recommend him to anyone seeking strong, reliable legal support.

reviews.text: As somebody who has had to seek resolution through an attorney on more than one occasion and have dealt with some of the biggest law firms in the chicago landing area, The difference between them and doug, is night and day. The feeling you get on first contact with a large firm is good and then you quickly find out It's more of a mill that turns over clients in high volume. And you are nothing special. The feeling I had with Doug was custom in nature and personal. Doug is not an ambulance chaser or firm that spends large amounts of money to embed themselves in companies that nudge you in their direction through kickbacks. (Yes this happens, I had inside knowledge and witnessed it at my former employer. Doug cares about one thing, actually two. Making sure you are taken care of throughout the process and making sure that you are fully compensated for any pain, suffering or inconvenience that you've had to experience. He's extremely thoughtful in his process, He's not worried about quickly settling things so that he can collect a check, He makes sure that every angle it's covered when it comes to your care during the process and after. I can't tell you how refreshing it is to deal with a lawyer as ethical and experienced as doug. This is why, even though I've been offered some pretty nice kickbacks to push people to these large firms, I send them to Doug for absolutely nothing, because I care about the people close to me getting the best representation that I believe they can get. Doug IS that guy.
reviews.ownerResponse: It was an honor to represent you and we are always here to help with a legal matter, whenever you need us. Best wishes always, to you and your family.

reviews.text: I Called to get help and Neil Good was rude when I answer the phone didn’t say hello this Neil good from just start asking questions! He over talked me I couldn’t get a word in very unprofessional… wasn’t trying to hear me out at all! He didn’t care that’s how he made me feel.. not good at all! I didn’t feel unheard he was just rude and just because someone has kids doesn’t mean they are at home playing house! It is a real struggle for me and on a day to day my kids are blessing!😊 just because someone looks good on the outside don’t mean they are on the inside! 😏 I’m not trying to live off the government an I didn’t ask to have MS! But all in all I will find someone to help me it’s just not y’all! 🤗 Thanks and have a blessed day!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi there, and thanks for sharing your experience. It seems Neil, my father, left quite the impression on you! 😄 Neil has a knack for jumping right into the thick of things, often out of eagerness to understand and potentially help. If you felt overwhelmed by his barrage of questions, we truly apologize. He's not testing your trivia knowledge, I promise!

Oftentimes, clients might not present us with the information we need upfront. This makes it essential for Neil to fire away those questions to figure out how and if he can champion your case. Just a heads-up, we do have a checklist for taking on disability cases. Items like consistent medication management, physical therapy, hospitalizations, and doctor's support throughout are critical. And, a fun fact: if someone's home starting and actively raising a family, that's something social security often sees as being able to work. Perhaps that's why Neil is always so inquisitive! 😉

Again, we're sorry you felt unheard, and we hope this sheds some light on our approach. All the best!

Sincerely,

Shawn Good

So, first, I'll say that I typically don't really write reviews.  Maybe I've written two in my lifetime.  I don't have the time and it's not normally my thing.  That said I am taking the time because I think it's vital that people know about Shawn Good.

Without going into too much detail, Shawn represented my daughter in a disability case.  My daughter is in her late 20's and has suffered terribly her whole life with mental illness.  It has been a tragedy for our whole family.  Because of mental illness my daughter has made mistakes and the court system does not understand nor is it very tolerant at all with people who suffer with mental illness. (That's a whole other speech.)

If you yourself are mentally ill or have a loved one who is, you know mental illness is a unseen, misunderstood and debilitating handicap. It is completely misunderstood by the general public and people are penalized repeatedly because they are mentally ill.  Help is so hard to find.

Shawn represented my daughter so she could be declared disabled (I'm unfamiliar with all the legal terms) and he won the case.  Now, the fact he won, alone, is a huge big deal.  While it certainly won't make my daughter rich nor even support her, it will help her to at least to have a fresh start.

You may think that's enough but Shawn did so much more...

Let me explain my perception and experience with Shawn in the very brief time I have known him.  He is a passionate advocate for the mentally ill.  Today he met us at court, on his own dime, to support my daughter during her criminal case.  He wasn't asked to do this, he offered.

Shawn has gone way, way above the call of duty and has been genuine, honest, sincere and deeply caring to my daughter, me and our situation.  He's been professional, prompt and fair...way more than fair.  His staff have been down-to-Earth, respectful, professional and kind.

I do hesitate saying some of this because sometimes if a person is too nice they are sometimes taken advantage of and I'd hate to see that happen to Shawn simply because he's one of those rare attorney's (in my opinion) who care deeply and go well above and beyond the call of duty.

Today, when I saw Shawn in action...professional, deeply caring, courteous, respectful and loving (yes, I said loving about a lawyer...I know doesn't seem possible...) I said to him, "why are you doing this?"  He said (not exactly in his words) "because I hate to see people taken advantage of."  It was clear by what else he said that he wants justice for the underdog, it makes him furious when people are taken advantage of.

I told him that he should do something to change the way things are now for the mentally ill. I told him he needs to somehow change the laws, make things better for people like my daughter.  (Maybe I was thinking too big in that moment, maybe that was a long conversation we didn't have time for...) But he would be the guy.  Maybe some day he will...

All he asked in return for what he did for us was "can you write a google review?  That would really help."  So that's how and why I am writing this.  If you knew me you'd know I am an honest, forthright, professional business woman who has been around the block many times.  I run numerous small businesses and have a keen sense and intuition about people when I meet them.

Shawn is good people.  Good name for a good person.  I say this honestly and sincerely.  Thanks Shawn.  Meeting a young person like Shawn gives me just a spark of hope for our future.
Very very very expensive...$200 per consultation...for the first time!!!!

His receptionist is snooty and arrogant.   He wasted 2 years of my time and as my new attorney confirmed,  this joke basically did nothing with my case.  So glad I fired him!

I AM THE SINGLE MOM WHO WAS TAKEN ADVANTAGE OF BY K. DEVANEY.

Let me start by saying: stay 100 miles away from this man and his office!!! My experience with him was nothing short of a nightmare. I turned to him for legal help during an incredibly challenging time, believing he could be trusted. However, his actions soon proved otherwise.

At one point, I needed him to do me a favor and act as my representative to guarantee a certain amount of money so I could secure housing. Ken assured me that transferring a large sum of money into his personal account was a safe and practical option. He was paid $5,000 for this task, and I trusted him to handle the funds responsibly. However, shortly after the transfer, he arrived with a brand-new Mercedes, which immediately raised my suspicions.

When I later requested official bank statements to see how he was handling my money, he failed to provide them. Eventually, when I demanded the remaining funds be returned, he handed me a check that was $18,000 short. Despite having all the receipts and evidence, he blatantly lied about the accuracy of the amount. This was nothing less than theft, exploiting the trust I placed in him as my lawyer.

To make matters worse, when I reported him to the ARDC and provided all the evidence, he retaliated by exposing personal details he was professionally obligated to keep confidential. Instead of addressing the issue, he used those details to deflect blame and paint me as the problem. This was a disgusting and unethical move from someone whose job is to uphold the law and protect their clients.

Ken Devaney is not someone you can trust. His actions were deceptive, manipulative, and entirely self-serving. He exploited my vulnerable position and failed to fulfill his professional and ethical duties.

If you’re considering hiring him, DON’T. Protect yourself and your loved ones from someone who has no respect for ethical boundaries or the trust placed in him as a lawyer. Learn from my experience and stay far, far away.

P.S. I have all the evidence to support everything I’ve written here. If anyone is interested, feel free to leave a comment below.

reviews.text:
I'm writing this review on behalf of my Aunt in Clinton MD. She is a widow who worked tirelessly to take care of her husband who had cancer. When she got into an accident a year ago she had NO idea that the law firm that she would choose would treat her like just a number, without compassion, and not work for HER best interest. But I did. If she would have called me first and asked me about my experience with this law firm I would have told her to RUN. Do not deal with them because based on my wife and I experience with them, especially the Illinois office, it was not a good experience. However, she had already committed and was deeply involved for longer than allowed. There problem was because she didn't do therapy right away, they couldn't really do much to get the money THEY wanted. She couldn't go to therapy because her husband was going through cancer treatments that took up most of her time amongst other things that divided her attention. Now she's suffering hip problems due to the accident and they say it's too late they want to close the case and on top of that split her settlement between two attorneys in the office. One of which just got handed her case just weeks ago. She's been passed around to several attorneys already just to be told she's not worth anymore of their time. Let's get her of the books and move on to the next number that can get us money seems to be the attitude of this law firm. Trust me I'm speaking from experience. I didn't give them a good review when I dealt with them. I'm doing this review for my aunt because she's older and just recently buried her husband. This was not a good experience for her. If I could give 0 stars I would. My aunts name is Cora Brown-Miller. She gave me her permission to write this review and give her name.

Concerned,
Nephew

He ultimately withdrew from the case before completing the work he was hired to do, leaving my brother without representation. Despite the significant fee paid, we have not received reimbursement for the unused portion of the retainer.

This was our experience, and I encourage others to carefully consider their options when choosing legal representation.

My abusive ex-husband had convinced everyone from my neighbors to DCFS to the police that I was mentally unstable. So when he stabbed me and told me I’d never see my son again, I was the one who was arrested and charged with a felony. Meanwhile, he sat around chitchatting with my neighbors and the police officers about how “crazy” I am. He walked away with an OP against me AND my 5 year old son.
With his insurmountable level of knowledge and experience, Mr. Wysocki saw past all of the lies and allegations against me and saw me as I truly am. He was initially successful in getting the charge dismissed. Unfortunately, the state’s attorney was determined to “put me in jail” and subsequently filed 2 felony charges against me. Al and Daniel Wysocki worked tirelessly on my case, combing through every detail. Neither of them believed that I would stage such a farce. Al and Daniel Wysocki were the only ones who stood by me for nearly 3 years before I was found not guilty. For that, I will be forever grateful to both Mr. Albert and Daniel Wysocki.

Marjorie and her staff are not only extremely professional but also very understanding and supportive. I found that unlike other attorneys in her field who want to simply tell you what they know and want you to do, Marjorie truly listened to me and what I wanted. This was the most important thing to me in our dealings. I have recommended Marjorie to several people and will continue to do so with the utmost confidence that she will do a superb job in representing what each person wants. She is a great attorney and comes highly recommended by me.

I highly recommend Marjorie. She is an honest, extremely knowledgeable attorney who truly cares about doing what is best for the clients she represents and is deeply committed to the well-being of every child involved. She is direct and straight forward. Her understanding of the law clearly stands out, and she is always transparent with her fees, providing detailed monthly breakdowns. She consistently comes to court fully prepared and well versed in every aspect of the case. Her office staff is also wonderful.

I have worked with Marjorie for many years through high-conflict and stressful post-divorce matters, and she has consistently demonstrated integrity, professionalism, and strong ethics. I always knew my child’s best interests were being fought for, which was the most important thing to me. When I was first searching for representation, other attorneys in Lake County also recommended Marjorie, which speaks volumes. Having experience with other attorneys, I can confidently say her pricing is in line with what other high-quality professionals charge, and she takes great pride in her work.

Hiring Marjorie was one of the best decisions I’ve made, and I would not hesitate to reach out to her again or recommend her services to others.

Marjorie is extremely biased and will drain your finances before you even see a day in court. She bills endlessly for phone calls, emails, and miscellaneous updates while doing almost nothing to move your case forward. Meanwhile, your ex-spouse can proceed in court with full representation while you’re left paying for nothing.

I have never seen a lawyer cut someone off so quickly during the most difficult and intense parts of a divorce—just because she wasn’t continuing to get her thousands of dollars for no progress. Court dates kept getting scheduled not to move the case forward, but so she could keep sending emails, making calls, and running up the bill while delivering no real results.

She constantly pressures you for more money, lectures you, and makes you feel like you’re not doing enough, even though she is the one supposed to be handling everything on your behalf. If you refuse or run out of money, she can even put liens on your home or other assets, making it nearly impossible to hire another lawyer.

Marjorie is completely unethical. She sees clients as nothing more than cash to be mined. She has no compassion, no professionalism, and no concern for what happens to you, your children, or your life. Her only priority is making sure she gets paid, no matter the cost to your case, your divorce, or your family.

If you value your money, your sanity, and the well-being of your children, stay far away from this lawyer.

I retained Marjorie for representation in an extremely high-conflict, post-decree custody matter. From the outset, her professionalism, thoroughness, and strategic insight were evident. She consistently demonstrated a thoughtful approach, took the time to listen, and responded to every inquiry with clarity and timeliness—never leaving me to feel that my case was unimportant.

Marjorie came to every communication and court appearance exceptionally well-prepared. Her depth of experience showed in how she analyzed complex issues, anticipated challenges, and navigated each step of the process with precision. She is also extremely organized, which brought clarity and calm to what was otherwise a daunting and overwhelming experience. Throughout, I always felt supported and effectively advocated for.

She brought the experience and guidance I needed during a very difficult time. In working with Marjorie, I can now say I feel my child is safe—and that his needs have been heard. I only wish I had found her sooner.

Marjorie,
Thank you for your prompt reply — ironically, it's the fastest we've ever received from you, and unfortunately, it came only after we posted a negative review. It’s a shame that it required public criticism for you to respond with urgency.
I'd like to address several points in your response to clarify and provide context:
1. Client Relationship , You stated you did not represent me, yet for over two years we spoke several times — both over the phone and in person — and you accepted payments from me indirectly. That clearly establishes a client relationship. Furthermore, you contacted me on my personal cell phone, which I can prove with a screen shot. My fiancé could not afford your legal fees on her own, as her ex-husband has never contributed a single dollar toward their children’s six years of college expenses. I stepped in financially, and you were well aware of that. To now claim otherwise is deeply misleading.
2. Our Complaint Is Not About the Judge’s Ruling,  Let me be clear — our issue is not with the court’s decision, but with your legal representation. We came to you on the advice of a trusted attorney, expecting you would deliver the same quality service you provided to others. Sadly, that was not the case. You consistently failed to protect our interests. The core issue — proving that my fiancé’s ex-husband did not fulfill his financial obligations for college tuition — was not properly addressed by you, despite us providing all necessary documentation. The fact that he had already been jailed for the same issue, and was in default per a previous court order, makes this failure inexcusable.
3. Our Review Is Honest, Not Vengeful  This isn’t about vengeance — it’s about accountability. We are warning others not to endure the same emotional and financial toll that we experienced under your representation. A simple case dragged on for over two years and cost us a significant amount of money, with nothing to show for it.
4. Responsibility for the Case Outcome , The judge ruled based on the evidence — or lack thereof — that you presented. You had all the tools and documentation to demonstrate the non-payment of college tuition, and you failed to do so. This was your primary task, and your failure to execute it directly impacted the case.
5. A Stark Contrast to Your Public Image , We’re genuinely perplexed by your abundance of 5-star reviews. In our experience, your demeanor was dismissive and, at times, condescending. Conversations often felt like interrogations. Whether we were treated this way due to prejudice or were simply viewed as a paycheck, the fact remains: your services were profoundly inadequate.
You were entrusted with not only a legal case but the well-being and future of two children. That trust was misplaced. You did not serve your clients with the diligence and compassion your profession demands. We are 110% dissatisfied and feel obligated to share our experience to protect others from enduring the same.
Our hope is that this serves as a moment of reflection for you. Legal representation is about more than billable hours — it’s about advocacy, integrity, and humanity. I sincerely hope you one day recognize the consequences of your actions on the families you serve.

Please save your inaccurate and phony response!

Good luck to you!

After a year-long, emotionally draining legal battle, I can confidently say that hiring Marjorie Sher was the best decision I made. As a father living out of state, I faced what seemed like impossible odds in reconnecting with my two children. The case was complex and filled with obstacles, but Marjorie never backed down. She worked tirelessly—often outside of regular hours—to ensure every detail was covered and every filing was timely.

What sets Marjorie apart is not just her legal expertise, but her unwavering communication and dedication. She kept me informed at every step, explaining the legal process in clear, compassionate terms, and never left me feeling in the dark. Her ability to build trust and maintain strong professional relationships with judges, opposing counsel, and court personnel was instrumental in achieving a positive outcome.

Thanks to her relentless advocacy and deep understanding of family law, I am now reunited with my children. Marjorie not only gave me a legal victory—she gave my family a second chance. I’ll forever be grateful for her hard work, integrity, and heart. If you’re looking for a lawyer who truly cares and delivers results, look no further.

reviews.text:
Disappointment in Marjorie’s services is an understatement for how I feel. The past two years of being represented by her have been an absolute nightmare. We were referred to Marjorie through a mutual friend who had a brief experience with her—one in which her true services were not revealed. I wish I could turn back time to erase this experience and instead choose a qualified, morally guided human being.

I hired Marjorie to uphold a previous court ruling from my divorce decree. To make a long story short, what seemed like a straightforward case lasted two years and was heard by four different judges. Additionally, during those two years, she failed to protect my assets related to the case—a simple motion that, I later learned from other attorneys, could have been filed to safeguard them. She never even mentioned this option to me, which I believe was negligent.

Over two years, I had only a handful of calls with Marjorie to discuss my case. Every time a call was scheduled, her office would inform me that another retainer was due, as she had already exhausted the previous one with busy work. Imagine how that felt for two years with absolutely no progress. Marjorie approached every conversation as if I were the criminal in the case. She spoke down to me, questioned my information, and offered no helpful strategies for moving forward. I often had to remind her of important details I had already provided to her. There was no sense of care or loyalty. On numerous occasions she would ask me, “What do you want to do?”—which I found baffling since I hired her as my attorney to advise me! So unprofessional.

Not only was she unsuccessful in my case, but she also blamed me for not asking her to seek clarity from the judge within 30 days of the ruling on issues I had already explained to her and supported with evidence in my exhibits binder. She had everything she needed to help me be successful, but chose not to.

To top it off, in our last phone call—where she again used a rude tone—she told me she didn’t agree with one part of the judge’s ruling that was in my favor (a small benefit, I might add). She literally said she didn’t think I should have received it! I had her repeat herself twice to me because I couldn’t believe what I was hearing. Marjorie, who I paid $40,000 over 2 years and lost my case, was not happy for this small benefit to me. This was a slap in the face to me. Absolutely pitiful behavior.

I hope my two-year nightmare doesn’t become someone else’s. If you’re looking for an attorney to stand by your side with loyalty and provide professional guidance, Marjorie is definitely NOT the one. Not only did I lose my case, but I also feel betrayed. It’s an awful feeling I hope others never have to experience.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your review.  I'm sorry that you were unhappy with the Judge's ruling after trial and what you perceive was my role in that end result, but I appreciate you bringing these issues to my attention.  I apologize if you felt that my office requesting that you pay an advance retainer prior to trial put a burden on you in what was an already stressful time.  As to your case spanning over two years and having four separate Judges assigned to your case, please know that your situation was an exception with unique issues.  Family law cases, by their very nature, are unpredictable.  As you can see from my other reviews, I am known for providing exemplary service, caring about my clients and their outcomes and listening to their thoughts about their own case and I am sorry that you feel that you did not receive that service.

reviews.text:
⭐ 1 Star
We have had an extremely disappointing experience with Marjorie regarding a child support case. From the start, it was clear that her main priority was billing as many hours as possible rather than achieving a resolution. Our case was unnecessarily dragged out for over two years, with little to no progress being made, and communication was frustratingly slow. Marjorie would always call and remind us of an invoice that is being sent instead of focusing on the case and she always made us feel like we were the defendants in our case when it was us who hired her to pursue my fiances ex husband who won’t support his children financially) Instead of advocating for our best interests, I felt like we were just another paycheck. If you’re looking for an attorney who genuinely cares about your case and works efficiently, I would strongly recommend looking elsewhere.

PS, We are also in the process of filing a formal complaint against Marjorie with the Illinois ARDC, as we have identified multiple valid reasons to do so as she is totally unethical and does not work in favor of her clients. Unfortunately, this step has become necessary, but we feel it is important to take action to prevent others from experiencing the same issues we did. There are many attorneys that will work to your benefit and Marjorie is NOT one of them!

reviews.text: I cannot speak to the gentleman who posted on this review below me however, I obtained this attorney roughly 4 years ago and as a father and as a man she represented me amazingly! I have equal time with my kids….I literally see my kids every other day. She will fight for you regardless if you’re a dad or mom. You obtain her because she’s damn good at what she does and will fight tooth and nail for you. Haven’t ever regretted obtaining her and continue to use her 4 years later when small things pertaining to my divorce arise.

These types of attorneys are why active fathers lose custody and time with their children.  My children mom attained this lawyer and from the beginning even with knowing the facts attempted to discredit the type of father I was to my two girls.   She had no compassion or best interest for my children.  She was attempting to have my time reduced even with knowing for a fact the mom wasn’t telling the truth.  In the end Justice prevailed and I got time with my children  She’s a waste of money and would have you thinking you can lie and come out on top.  Which in my case her client didn’t…….
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Ruff - I did not represent you, but instead the mother of your children.  Clearly you were unhappy with the end result of the case, as it was not the outcome that you desired.  The Mother of your children was happy with my representation, and I provided exemplary service to her by advocating her position in this case.  The Guardian Ad Litem was charged with protecting  your children's interests, and she did so with honor and grace and approved the final documents which gave you less time than you wanted.   As to your comment that I am a waste of money, I do not know how you would come to that conclusion, as I did not represent you.

reviews.text: In 2012, I first retained Marjorie Sher as the attorney to help me through the divorce process. Over the decade since, I have returned to her law office numerous times to utilize her services for custody, maintenance, and other various issues that came up during that interim. Marjorie was always professional. She always presented my options and gave her spot on recommendations. I always felt like Marjorie had my back. Her office staff has consistently been top-notch. While I continue to feel fortunate that I selected her as my attorney, I also want my review to include how much I appreciated her as a human being. Marjorie is a genuinely kind and compassionate person.

I would highly recommend Marjorie's services to anybody in need of a divorce attorney. She was recommended to me by my friend who used her services. I reached out to her and she represented me for my divorce and was literally the best. She kept me informed of every step along the way whether it was from what she was doing or what my ex's side was doing. She is very professional and gave me the best advice that would help me. When I expressed my situation that I was running low on money from the legal actions and fees, she thought of ways to help me save. She is very concerned about the well being of her clients and again....5 stars. Not one bad thing to say or not ONE complaint.
reviews.ownerResponse: I appreciate your feedback and kind words.  Family Law Cases are difficult, emotionally and financially.  I’m glad I made a difficult time in your life a little bit easier.

reviews.ownerResponse: I have reported this review to google as it is factually inaccurate and even inconsistent with itself. Clients are always instructed to wait for their attorney to check in. This is an instruction I give to all of my clients because I have multiple cases on my docket and need time to deal with each accordingly. In every instance, a judge will not call a defendant without their attorney present unless they are under the impression that the person does not have an attorney. This means the client checked in against my instructions and then voluntarily stepped before the judge without informing them that they had retained an attorney. This review states on multiple occasions that I never "showed up" then states that I was there but late.

I sustained a spinal cord injury in a bicycling accident in Texas, which left me paralyzed from the chest down. The insurance company that provided coverage for the cycling club that I was in refused to pay more than a nominal amount for my medical care even though the club’s policy was for up to $1,000,000.

Michael Viglione of Ryan, Ryan and Landa, represented me in obtaining an equitable settlement with the insurance company. Initially, the insurance company refused to negotiate. After exploring a variety of options, Mr. Viglione partnered with a firm in Texas to move forward with a suit against the insurance company even though partnering with another firm meant sharing any potential fee.

The insurance company filed a motion to have the suit dismissed. When the judge ruled to allow the suit to move forward, the insurance company agreed to mediation. Through the mediation process, we quickly came to terms with the insurance company.

The settlement from the insurance company alleviated the financial of burden of the ongoing medical, rehabilitation and home care costs of being a paraplegic.  Thanks to the settlement, I can live independently with minimal assistance and without imposing a financial burden on my daughters and their families.

Reed Lee
Oak Park, IL

Everybody else that I worked with in the Salvi & Maher firm, including the administrative people and paralegals were all fantastic to work with.  They followed up on everything going on or let me know right away if they needed a document from me or needed to set up an interview or deposition with me.  The entire firm has great attitude, extremely friendly, and very caring...which really helps when trying to get through an extremely hard time in your life after an accident.

I would recommend anybody needing an attorney to represent them through an accident that may have occurred in an area that Salvi & Maher handles, give them a call right away.  You won't find a better group of people to help you get through your case and win for you.

Mrs. Jimenez-Martinez,

On December 4, 2024, you and The Gloria Law Group entered into an agreement where you agreed to pay the final outstanding invoices and the filing fee on the new case and Atty Rodriguez would represent you for free in the new case. What I found particularly troubling was their behavior during Zoom court sessions. I witnessed members of the team laughing while the judge was addressing me—something that felt incredibly disrespectful and out of place in such a serious setting.
The overall presentation and professionalism of the staff were far below expectations. Poor hygiene and lack of decorum contributed to an environment that felt careless and unprofessional. I would not recommend this firm to anyone looking for professional, ethical, and client-focused legal representation

Joe helped me in so many different ways. He helped me understand my rights, used his extensive experience to help guide me through every step of the process, and ultimately helped me secure residential custody of my son so that I could continue to send him to the school he has always attended. I would hate for anyone to have to go through something as stressful and hectic as a divorce, but if you find yourself in that situation you will be in good hands with Joe representing you. Hello, I'm sharing my experience with attorney Andrés Cerritos. On May 29th, my wife, Silvia Damian, had her appointment in Ciudad Juárez, and unfortunately, her visa was denied because this attorney didn't have her fingerprints checked. As a result, she was denied a visa for life, leaving behind a husband and four children. Our family was torn apart, and his explanation was that attorney Jerry asked my wife if she had been caught with any documents or anything like that, and since my wife said no, that's why he never had her fingerprints checked. I know someone who was never stopped by immigration, and they never took his fingerprints, yet his attorney still had them checked not once, but twice, to ensure there was nothing on his record for a guaranteed visa. I'm seriously considering suing him for negligence and filing a complaint with the Bar Association, and from there, a civil lawsuit. I swear on my children that this won't go unpunished. I will proceed legally, even if it takes time and money. I will never forgive them for separating my family. Go to another attorney. If I could give a rating... The lower the rate, the better, and don't pay him in cash, because a paid musician who doesn't play well... you think I'm ignorant, but you're very wrong.

reviews.ownerResponse: We definitely had some problems with communication about the time you wrote the review due to the lengthy and serious illness of a staff member.  We have adopted a team approach so that this never happens again.  All clients have several points of contact with our staff of highly professional legal assistants.  We can now pledge to return all calls and emails within 24 hours if humanly possible to do so.  I’m happy that after your review was written we went to trial and got a very successful result. There is no record that she ever filed a Workers’ Compensation case. We wish her all the best, and if she did have any legal issues, that they have been resolved to her satisfaction.

I contacted Al & his firm at the last second, and they quickly helped me alter my court date, instructed me on how to proceed, and then helped me win my case when it occurred.

Al, Sophie, and their team constantly texted me to update me and ensure I was informed and worry-free about the process.

On the day of my court appearance, Al personally came and explained the process and what I would need to do, guiding me the whole way.

If you have an upcoming court date, I suggest using Al & his team to fight for you. Luckily, thanks to them, my ticket didn't impact my driving record or insurance!

I rarely leave reviews, but this experience requires transparency.

My recent transaction involving Yolanda as the representing attorney for my seller client  was handled with a concerning lack of diligence, communication, and professional oversight. From contract to closing, there was minimal urgency and little evidence of structured file management.

Earnest money coordination was unnecessarily delayed and required repeated follow-up. Basic transactional milestones were not proactively managed. At one point, it became clear that the buyer did not have proper legal structure or coordination in place — something that should have been identified and addressed immediately by counsel.

Communication was reactive rather than directive. Deadlines were not clearly controlled, and key steps in the process required intervention from parties outside her role. Title coordination for closing lacked organization and leadership, resulting in avoidable confusion and inefficiency.

Throughout the transaction, I found myself stepping into responsibilities that typically fall within the attorney’s scope — ensuring compliance, clarifying contractual terms, and pushing the file forward to prevent collapse. That should not be necessary in a professionally managed transaction. Based on this experience, I cannot recommend her services. Respectfully.
Not easy to work with.
Attentive and returned calls right away.
She was great with closing on a house I bought.
A super good lawyer with a lot of experience and a great team, she always fights for the best, whether buying or selling houses. 1000% recommended.

I'm truly grateful unto you and thank you so much for leading me, guiding me on my first time homebuyer's journey. I didn't know what to expect, when by chance I met a superb realtor while taking pictures of a home that was for sale it had the realtor's name/picture of Armando Popoca (yoooooooo Armando bro, what up? lol). After numerous searches for homes all over Waukegan, he found a home to my liking. After deciding move forward with the purchase. I didn't know what to do at all. So, he suggested an attorney and lo and behold, this GEM of a law firm, he introduced me to!! Listen, please, call and utilize this law office. The offices, conference rooms, coffee and doughnuts are EXCELLENT. So, as I close for fear of running out of space! I would recommend the Law Office of Mayra Prado-Pagan to anyone who is looking for supreme first-class legal services, which are absolutely worth the time, and the dime. You get what you pay for, so pay for the best! THE BEST! Gracias! Que Dios te bendiga, amen. Thank you all again...

Mr. T. Davis

I worked with Michael, who proved to be extremely cooperative and communicative throughout the entire process, ensuring I remained well-informed and confident in the progress to get my family trust done.

My husband and I loved our experience here! We were drawing up estate documents, which I kept thinking would be an overwhelming process, but Michael Goldberg made it very easy. We had an initial Zoom call for him to get information. He was very easy to get a hold of to ask questions. They drew up the documents, and we went in to sign and ask last-minute questions. It was a pleasant experience working with Mr. Goldberg and staff. I will definitely recommend the firm to my friends and family.

reviews.text: Big thanks to Julia and the Spitzer Law team! They were professional, organized, and incredibly supportive. Julia always took the time to answer my questions. I highly recommend them to anyone needing legal help.

Highly recommend Julia + Jeremy from Spitzer Law PC. We’ve worked with them for the past year on a civil litigation case and won both a motion for summary judgement and sanctions motion thanks to their hard work and knowledge. Julia and Jeremy have been extremely patient, helpful and giving us the best guidance as we navigated a very stressful situation. They’ve been very professional and personable always making sure we felt supported and up to date with everything through the process, even working with myself as I’ve been out of the country for most of the process. If you’re looking for an amazing legal team I highly recommend Spitzer Law!
The best team! They've helped me with a house sale and purchase in the last year. Quick and helpful responses and personable. Highly recommend!

I was so impressed with Jeremy's knowledge and understanding of my case. Julia and Zandria were very pleasant to work with. They always kept me informed of the progress. Thanks to their expertise, I was able to achieve a positive outcome that exceeded my expectations. I am beyond grateful!!!

I cannot recommend Julia and her team enough. I had a high conflict, and long divorce, that was handled with precision, professionalism, and detail. Julia matched my personal style when it comes to communication, and efficiency, making the process easier knowing we were on the same page. I would highly recommend the Spitzer law team and am grateful for their help and direction.

I cannot speak highly enough about the exceptional legal team at Spitzer Law, Jeremy Spitzer and Julia Keenan. For the past 15 years, I’ve been continually impressed by their professionalism, expertise, patience and genuine dedication to my case.

Jeremy’s & Julia’s depth of legal knowledge and their ability to explain legal matters in an easily understandable way provided me with both clarity and confidence throughout the entire process. They’re both incredibly strategic, always thinking several steps ahead, which gave me peace of mind knowing I was in such capable hands.

Both are equally amazing in that not only are they highly skilled but also compassionate and approachable. They took the time to listen to my concerns, ensuring I always felt supported. Their responsiveness and attention to detail were remarkable, making me feel like a priority every step of the way.

Together, Jeremy and Julia make an incredible team, demonstrating a perfect blend of legal prowess and client care. Their tireless effort, unwavering commitment, and clear communication made all the difference in achieving a favorable outcome for me.

If you’re looking for lawyers who are not only highly skilled but also truly care about your case, I highly recommend Spitzer Law. You won’t find a better team!

reviews.text: I spent almost 500k during a messy 2 year divorce that had no end in sight. Lake county is not friendly nor fair to affluent Caucasian male fathers...it is a travesty. Jeremy was able to helps us navigate to resolution within 3 sessions. I HIGHLY recommend Jeremy for even handed mediation.

Jeremy, Julia, and Zandria were exceptional to work with. The patience and professionalism they exhibited was profound. Jeremy, without missing any details, handled my case as quickly as possible. I highly recommend this firm to handle your legal needs.

I am extremely grateful to Julia for her expert legal advice and help with navigating my case which involved discrimination in the workplace. It was a stressful time, but Julia made the whole process a breeze. She was very communicative, kept me abreast of the status of my case and delivered an outcome that was beyond my expectations. I truly enjoyed working with Julia and I would highly recommend Spitzer Law for any employment law matters.
Jeremy and Zandria are extremely helpful and quick to respond to any questions I have. They were amazing in helping me finalize my divorce and made a difficult time in my life as easy as possible.

Julia Keenan did an awesome job for us! I don’t usually write reviews, but we were so impressed with her attention to detail and thoroughness.  Her input saved us time and money. We would highly recommend her services.

I cannot express enough gratitude for the outstanding legal assistance Julia provided. We were involved in a fraudulent contract with a car dealership. Julia navigated the complexities with great professionalism and thoroughness.
Her communication was excellent, consistently prompt and detailed. She had an in-depth understanding of the legalities surrounding our case and fought tirelessly for us. Her confidence and commitment was reassuring in what could have been a stressful ordeal.
I wholeheartedly recommend Julia Kennan to anyone in need of legal representation. Thank you, Julia, for advocating for us and  delivering precisely the outcome we had hoped for.
Jeremy is a pleasure to work with. He’s knowledgeable and talks to you in terms you will understand. I would recommend him 100 percent.

I would highly recommend Spitzer Law.

Mr. Jeremy goes on and beyond making sure all your needs be on point! friendly and understanding,

We are very happy with the outcome that Mr. Spitzer and Zandria helped us to obtain. They explained everything thoroughly. Our court hearing was more than I expected, but Jeremy got us through it and won the case!
I would highly recommend Spitzer Law.

Mr. Spritzer is an amazing person, he helped through our process thoroughly and with absolutely genuine knowledge of it all. He took the time to explain things and always returned our calls in a timely manner. His Paralegal Ms. Galvez is also the sweetest and very helpful with any questions that arose. The team in itself is extraordinary. I will definitely return if I ever needed legal help again.

Jeremy is the utmost professional, caring and honest lawyer I’ve ever had the good fortune to use. He listened to my concerns in my case and turned the tides literally changing the lives of myself and my children for the better in quick fashion. He’s there when you need him and incredibly professional and talented. Thank you Jeremy!

I wish I had found Mr. Spitzer as my first attorney, I'm grateful that I found him in time to finish the job. There was an immediate sense of relief for me upon hiring him. After months of being blown off by my first lawyer, Mr. Spitzer took the time to listen to what I was saying, to really understand the nature of our adversary. In the midst of rants about all the awful things I had been dealing with, I would hear the sound of his keyboard clacking after I let out details that would actually have an impact on the case. I knew he was paying attention, and I knew he was able to cut through the noise that I myself was making. He was always very even keeled, always shot straight with me, and was always ready to point out the erroneous advice I had previously received. He never filled my head with unrealistic expectations, and he never let me get discouraged by the setbacks. He knew when to keep pushing, and he knew how and when to say that we'd gotten as far as we could. Nobody gets everything they want in these matters, and while some of the compromises I had to make were hard to swallow at first, what I walked away with was objectively the best case scenario for myself and my daughter. It's a small outfit, just him and one paralegal (Ms. Galvez). They were both easily accessible, both able to address any needs in a timely manner. I can't recommend them highly enough.

Thank you so much Mike and Katherine!
They were helpful, diligent, fast and precise! I would recommend them to everyone

DebtStoppers has been helping me they've been very patient with me during the time of need after losing a loved one they took their time and waited for me to grieve over my loved one and postpone going forward with paperwork that I needed to sign. They helped me in many ways they signed me up with a course about finances and learning how to balance your money. They even paid the course for me.
If anyone is thinking of filing bankruptcy or even getting help with balancing your money or financing, DebtStoppers is what I recommend they have gone above and beyond as well as fair prices
Thank you again Michael Mueller, Karina and all of the debtstoppers staff for all your help, patience, knowledge, support and advice.

"I was facing a very stressful situation after a vehicular accident in 2024 that put me at risk of driver’s license revocation. Attorney Gregory Nikitas was highly referred by a friend who have vouched that he will be the best fit for my legal needs.

For over a year, Attorney Gregory Nikitas navigated the court process for me with incredible skill. That level of rapport and 'charm' made a huge difference in how my case was handled. They have a way of commanding the room while remaining completely professional. He would also make sure that in the event he was not available that he would have his partner represent me.

Thanks to their persistence, expertise and reputation, I walked away with my license intact. After a year of court dates, I couldn’t have asked for a better advocate in my corner. If you want someone who is genuinely connected and knows how to fight for you, I highly recommend McCollum Hodgkinson & Nikitas, LLC.  Hire someone who is best at what they do. It seems like I maybe an outlier here. Communication with Daniel was difficult for me and responses were often delayed even with multiple emails or phone calls.  I felt as though they knew it was an easy case so I was put on the backburner. We did get the desired outcome, but I placed this lower scoring due to communication and not properly setting up expectations.

The worst experience was a court date where I wasn't informed that my normal attorney would not be present and waited over 4 hours not knowing when representation would arrive at court. Every other case was heard while I waited, and they almost closed the session. Another attorney from the office arrived at closing to ask to be seen at another date (a wasted day for myself/my vacation time). What was more disappointing was that Daniel was not open to my feedback. I think he was confused that I didn't like that he wasn't there, but in reality it would have been fine if I knew what was going to happen. Instead the day of was filled with frantic calls from myself trying to figure out what was happening, and feeling anxious the entire time.

Dan was able to help us successfully navigate a very confusing and intimidating juvenile legal system. His experience and expertise gave us confidence and ultimately we were able to get our case dismissed entirely. This firm is worth every penny.

I was pulled over in February 2020 initially for speeding. Long story short, I was being taken to the Vernon Hills Police department for DUI. I had one previous DUI that was brought down to reckless driving 15 years ago. I knew that I was out of luck, and this time I would be convicted of DUI. I searched on Google for the best attorney for my situation, and McCollum Hodgkinson caught my eye and had great reviews, all for a good reason!!

I set up an appointment with Daniel Hodgkinson immediately. I was very nervous, but he reassured me that he and his firm would help. Daniel gave me information on what I need to get started right away to help my case. Seven months later and a few continuances, I received the fantastic news from Daniel himself, saying that he had done the impossible and reduced the charges to reckless driving!!!
I don't know how he did it, but I am glad he did. I would have lost my job if I were convicted of a DUI. I thoroughly recommend McCollum Hodgkinson!!!

Ronald Bell took the time to listen to every detail of my situation and clearly guided me on the best course of action.

Throughout the entire process, Ronald Bell demonstrated an impressive depth of knowledge and experience in family law. He left no stone unturned in preparing and executing my case, always ensuring that I was well-informed and comfortable every step of the way.

What truly sets Ronald Bell apart is his unwavering commitment to his clients. He was not just my lawyer; he became my advocate and a true source of support during what was without a doubt one of the most difficult and challenging times I have faced. His dedication to my case was evident in his tireless work ethic, attention to detail, and effective communication. Not to mention, he really does care!! A LOT!!!

When it came to negotiations and courtroom proceedings, Ronald Bell was a force to be reckoned with. His confidence and poise in the courtroom were remarkable, and it was evident that he had earned the respect of his peers (and everyone else in the courtroom).

Ultimately, the outcome of my case greatly exceeded my expectations, and it was all thanks to Ronald Bell. He fought tirelessly to protect my interests and secure the best possible future for my children and me.

I wholeheartedly recommend Ronald Bell to anyone facing family law issues. These situations are tough, and you need the very best by your side. He is not just an attorney, but a compassionate advocate, a legal genius, and a true champion for his clients. If you want the best possible outcome for your case, Ronald Bell is the lawyer you need.

Thank you Ron!!! For everything you've done for me and my family. You are a true legal superstar, and I am forever grateful.

This is probably long overdue.  Ron and his team facilitated my divorce over the course of a year.  Divorce is painful and emotional and if you're like me, you don't know much about the process and don't really have the headspace to learn all about it while it is happening to you...Ron's parting words at my first consult with him were, "The best thing you can do is pretend like you are asleep for the next 6-12 months and let me handle things."  Those words were so true.  I didn't always want to listen to them, but being 5 months post divorce and still dealing with some issues with opposing parties not living up to the terms of the MSA, Ron is still in my corner.  I don't think I realized how good he and his team are until recently.  Discussing my agreement with divorcees has shown me that Ron helped me reach a fair deal that allows me to move on in life with confidence and financial stability and do the right thing for my children and he kept emotion out of it which is so important in negotiation for separating assets and in my case a lot of liabilities.

I would absolutely recommend Mr. Bell and his team to anyone in need of advice and guidance and council during the process of divorce.

Lisa, Amy are all stars in their field- they are raising the bar and demonstrate significant accountability.

It’s hard to know where to start but here goes. This past year was an uphill battle for myself to try to get 50 percent custody of my two little ones due to an ugly divorce. In the beginning I googled the top lawyers around my area and thankfully I was able to find Ronald L. Bell & Associates P.C.. From the moment I walked in I knew I made the right choice. Lisa and Amy were there to not only listen to me but reassured me I had made the right choice to come and see Ron. Ron has been there for me through it all. He will tell you exactly what you need to do in order for you to get the results you want. I followed his instructions to a T. I did not sway at all even though sometimes I was frustrated with what was asked of me in court. He can be very stern and sometimes it comes off in a bad way but it comes from a place of experience. Deborah has just joined his team and she has been a great addition as well. She is very detailed in everything she does. I am more than happy to refer this firm to anyone who needs help because they are an awesome team!

Mr. Bell is an extremely smart and wise attorney. If you need legal representation, and you have the intelligence to quickly begin your legal process to expedite the situation, then Mr. Bell and his associates are, hands-down, the best legal team and/or representation you can attain. Listen to him and take the time to understand his instructions. He knows the system above a lot of other lawyers, because he has taken part in placing forth most of the laws to which we must abide. He knows the entire legal process and is extremely praised by his peers and judges in this area. To briefly explain, Mr. Bell has represented me in two separate cases. Both of the cases were led to desirable outcomes. This lawyer can and will help you. You just need to understand that with any lawyer or legal process, you must participate actively in your case to make any progression. Hiring a lawyer doesn’t mean he will read your mind and solve all your problems. Hiring a lawyer and his team just allows you to decrease the likelihood of your legal and financial deficit as a a result of your legal situation. Talk to him, take the time to understand everything, ask the questions you have, do as he says, communicate everything you can, and above all, do it in a timely manner and he will make sure you end up on the right side of your legal issue. He is by far the only lawyer I will ever contact, whether it be in Lake County, or if I have to fly him to any other corner of this earth, I will always use Mr. Bell and his associates for any legal matters I have at hand. There is no other legal representation I trust, except The Doctor of Law himself: Ronald L. Bell.
They have worked with me in my case, and he is the best lawyer in lake county. Lisa is awesome too! Ron is very knowledgeable and i would gladly recommend him.

I highly recommend Mr. Bell. He did a wonderful job winning my daughters case! If your looking for someone who will fight for you, he is the attorney for you! Did an excellent job, cant say enough about him.

Ronald and his staff’s performance and good character, in and out of the courtroom, left me with a forever positive impression resulting in my recommendation of Ronald Bell and Associates to anyone who has high standards for their attorney. If you are looking for an attorney, Mr. Ronald Bell will not let you down! I was not referred to him, I found him online, which made me skeptical, but I found the best attorney ever, and I now have an attorney which I will use when needed. Mr. Bell called outside the 9 to 5 day, which amazed me. His staff is top notch and will get you an answer quickly. I highly recommend Ronald Bell!

Ron has been my Attorney since August 30, 2016. I know that day very well. It's the first day since the day  my Husband left and took EVERYTHING out of our bank accounts (leaving myself and Children penniless) that I finally felt safe, protected and that I had a chance against that Man whom I thought I knew. Ron and his office Family have been with me every single step of the way. It's been a lot of steps too. My case still continues and they are still fighting for me. Ron is no lamb in the court room. He's a lion. That's what you want in a divorce case. He has seen everything in his career and will not let you get screwed over if he has anything to say about it. Trust me, he always does. You want someone who will be at dinner and still be thinking about your case? Ron is your guy. Want a 8-5 guy who leaves it at the door? Maybe he's not for you then. I'll always use Ron Bell and Associates. I deserve the best.

An absolute All-star and Professional!  Ron always takes the time to listen and then clearly educate you on the facts and next steps.  ALWAYS follow his direction and do exactly what he says given his decades of experience and legal expertise, and no matter how hard it may be for you personally, you will be fine in the end.  Helped me through the most difficult time in my life.  Said he would get me through my divorce with the best results and Ron and his team delivered (great amount of parenting time with kids as a Dad and settling things financially). I would highly recommend to any parent (Male or Female) going through a divorce. You need Ron Bell on your team.
Mr. Bell and his staff worked hard and did a fantastic job negotiating a settlement between my ex wife and my self. I plan to use him again for estate planning. Highly recommended.

Mr. Bell and his staff have been nothing but professional and attentive to my case, texting me updates and having all the time needed to address my concerns about my divorce.  Welcoming office and very friendly and helpful staff.  I'd definitely recommend him for any legal issues you might have.

I recently had some complications with my ex-husband and child support payments. After dealing with another attorney I was reluctant to hire one again. Ronald Bell and Doreen were great and put me at ease during a very difficult time. Mr. Bell is knowledgeable and fair, I was only charged for work done. My only regret was not finding them sooner. Thank you Ronald Bell and Associates!

Ron settled my divorce case and I could not have been more satisfied.

My ex and I had pretty much settled our case with one invaluable exception, the dog. After she broke our agreement and refused to give him back to me I spoke to multiple attorneys. Every singe one of them basically said "Why don't you just get another dog?" Until I met Ron. Ron understood exactly where I was coming from and sincerely cared that I get my dog back. The minute I hired him I felt like he truly cared about me and my situation. He was not just looking to settle the case, he was looking out for my best interest in getting the case settled. He was also realistic and told me he could not guarantee I would get my dog. He said based on the facts he felt I should, but he explained the process. It was not exactly what I wanted to hear, but he was honest and that was more important than an empty promise. I knew what to expect and that was very fair of him.

From years of experience Ron knew time was of the essence and he got the process started as quickly as he could. His team was amazing at letting me know how the process worked, where we were and what to expect next.

One of the reasons I felt so comfortable in the process was because of Ron's attentiveness. He listened to everything I had to say about the situation. He explained to me what was pertinent, what was not and explained why. He came up with some ideas on how to approach the situation and was willing to let me tell him what I was and was not comfortable with in regards to those ideas.

Ron's experience makes him very familiar with opposing attorney's and judges which is another fantastic asset. He knew the attorney my ex hired, even though she was from another county, which I thought was beneficial. He kept me informed of their conversations and always made sure I knew exactly where things stood.

Of the many, many strengths Ron possesses, I feel like his biggest strength is in the courtroom. I cannot imagine not having him on my side after seeing him in action and I would never want to see him on the opposing side.

When Ron walks into court you can just feel how respected he is by everyone. The judges, the courtroom employees and the other attorneys all show Ron a great deal of respect. People went out of their way to say hello to Ron and he just seemed at home in the courtroom.

The first time we were in court things got tense between my ex and me. Ron was very quick to come to my defense and call my ex out to her and her attorney when my she made a decision out of spite towards me. Because it was such an overwhelming and emotional situation it really put me at ease when he stepped up on my behalf.

When it came to the actual hearing Ron was at his absolute best. Because I had never been in court before he made sure I knew exactly what to expect. I felt much more at ease because of his help. He knew exactly what facts we needed to get across to the judge and made sure they were presented and supported. He was quick to get objections every time the opposing attorney and my ex made unreasonable remarks (like when she claimed to be able to know what the dog was thinking.)

When the judge ruled in my favor I did not feel like Ron's client. He was truly happy that I was going to get my dog back. When my ex and her attorney made snide comments he once again was quick to jump to my side.

What happened after the case solidified my beliefs of just how sincere Ron and his team were about my case. The night of the hearing I received a call from Ron asking if my dog was home and how he was doing. The case was over, we won. But he went out of way to check in on us and that made me feel like I was not just another client, he really cared.

At the end of the day I was completely satisfied with Ron's representation. When he took the case he got to know all the facts and he represented the absolute best anyone could. He used the facts and his experience to make sure we got the outcome we desired.

Mr. Bell's negotiation skills are not to be reckoned with!
I was stopped on US Highway 55 for an accident that was in front of me with nowhere to go when I was rear ended by two vehicles. I had a very difficult and complicated case because of the two other motorists. The wanted separate claims and jurisdiction. I had many injuries (previous, related and unrelated). I would not have received hardly any compensation for my injuries without Mr. Bell’s help. But we won with tremendous results. I was impressed with Mr. Bell’s knowledge, straight forwardness, honesty, and his negotiation skills are not to be reckoned with! He is one who plays to the letter of the law to build a strong foundation. He and his exceptional staff worked very hard to gather all medical and personal witnesses to attest to my character and the changes that had occurred in me. I always felt taken care of and respected by not only Mr. Bell but also his entire staff. I wouldn't hesitate to recommend him.

My husband and I had Ron work up our estate planning and power of attorney documents, something I put off way too long.  Ron came highly recommended by a close friend.  He made the process I feared as smooth as glass.  He took us through the legal process step by step and now I don't have to worry about how things will be handled during those potential stressful times.  I highly recommend Ron without reservation for your legal matters.

There are lawyers and there are lawyers that become your friend, family, advocate and triumphant

Loud and mean and combative does not equate to a better lawyer, outcome or to Ron bells style.

Ron bell is about settling your case for and with the benefits of the children first, you second as I feel it should be.

Understand, though Ron is far from a push over, I would not want to be on the opposing side that played unfair or dirty against him.

My case is unique for my custody case dealt with a special needs child that has a profound cognitive deficit along with medical issues.

However, I felt as well as Ron that the preponderance of evidence was in my favor to be awarded the vast majority of visitation, even sole custody, Ron looked at the bigger long-term picture and tried hard to facilitate a fair, and equitable settlement for all that would best suit my special child.

Even with a loud, egotistical and arrogant IMHO opposing attorney, Ron handled himself professionally, though threw and connected his punches when appropriate and stifled me when I went off on the opposing attorney (he, the opposing attorney deserved it).

Most important to you regarding this review is I walked away with the vast majority of time with my child, eliminated all the financial issues and can meet with my gods knowing it was handled properly.

Now I have my Child full-time where he is safe, happy and able to be himself!

Ron never lost any of the battles and won the case hands down.

The next most important consideration, fees!

Ron’s bill for handling the last 5 months of this 9-month case was less than two other lawyers with room to spare. Ron only bills for time worked on the case and Ron took my calls 24x7, and I mean this. He or his office would return my calls and texts in minutes or hours and even sooner, when I said it was an emergency

I know most other lawyers would have billed me 50,000 or more while my total bill was a fraction of this amount. Ron attended four way meetings, status calls, hearings, time with witnesses and numerous face-to-face meetings with me as well as several calls.

Bottom line I still receive texts and calls from Ron and his office just asking how things are going. I know they sincerely care.

Therefore, in my opinion after an ugly contested divorce 10 years back, other custody battles, and this final contested custody battle I am glad I found Ron Bell, I, and my special needs child sincerely recommend Ron bell to represent you and your family for all matters of law.

I have used Ron Bell for several legal matters and find him to be extremely knowledgeable and thorough to get the job done right.  Having an attorney that is experienced in several areas makes him the go to person when you need solid legal advice.

I have referred Ron to several people when they needed good legal representation and feel confident that he can do the job.  I would definitely want to have a good attorney that takes the stress out of any legal issues with the confidence knowing that he will take care of business.

Ron is very well known and involved within the legal community and find it amazing the number of people that he has come in contact with with his legal contacts and affiliations.  I would highly recommend Ron Bell to anyone who needs good legal advice.

Ron is an exceptional lawyer. I was impressed the first time we meet and he has followed through to the letter for any and all of my requests. As a single father it is important to me to see as much of my daughter as possible and with Ron's help I am able to get the law on my side. I was able to work out all the arrangements with her mother and I am happy to say we are 1 year drama free. I really appreciated all of Ron's expertise and would highly;y recommend his services for anyone seeking a divorce or child arrangements.

reviews.text: 5 stars does not do this team justice... I retained Denis in a time of need when I had been left feeling let down by previous representation. However, the very first time I had spoken with Denis, I knew this was the right move for me and I felt confident in providing the retainer and instilling my trust in Denis and team. You never get everything you want but I will say that I left with nothing taken from me or feeling defeated. The entire process with Denis and his team was as seamless as it could be given the circumstances of a lawsuit. Denis is a very compassionate, selfless, and trustworthy man. He was fair and understanding when it came to payments and timelines with finances. There were many reviews I read when choosing firms, but you can never be sure. I promise you with this team, you can be sure you are putting your legal matters into the right hands. Denis and his team were supportive, understanding, and kind; they fought fairly for me. When you are enduring tough family litigation, the stress is through the roof and Denis really helped to see me through this. I could not have accomplished what I did without this team. If you are looking for representation with empathy and understanding, please look no further. This is your team. They truly care for their clients and I can't say that I have ever felt cared for or that I have ever truly trusted an attorney until working with Gravel & Associates. Thank you for everything Denis & Team.

reviews.text:
Don't recommend. It seems that the only good attorney left that firm. I began working with this firm last yr regarding my divorce. My case was taken by Sara Robers and Mr. Gravel. They were both amazing and got my case going right away. As my case progressed my work was mainly with Ms. Roberts. She was amazing and supportive but unfortunately left the firm sometime in Oct/Nov 2023 and my case was transferred to Jocelyn Gravel Wechter. From the minute Ms. Roberts left my experience became disappointing. To begin with, I was not informed by the firm that Ms. Roberts left or who was going to be taking over my case. Evetually I had to request a meeting with, who ever was taking over, due to concerns with case. That is when I had my first interaction with Jocelyn. She was so apathetic and rude. She made me feel like my concerns were not valid and a bother. She proceeded to recommend I submit a request for temporary child support due to "potentially having case still take up to 6 months." When Ms. Roberts had previously informed me that it could be a few more months until finalizing divorce. All contact became so unprofessional, even with other paralegals. Constantly being told they didn't know my case and during this time I was also cc'd to emails of someone else's case (this included privet information with time and dates of meeting to transfer valuable items). The firm had more contact with me regarding my outstanding bill than my actual case. I attempted to work with them and expressed my concers to Mr. Gravel directly. He seems to be really concerned with my outstanding balance stating "disappointed with you for not making a payment." He also appeared to excuse all other concerns but even than I still agreed to a meeting with Jocelyn (after being informed that it was a no charge meeting). This meeting didn't occur due to Jocelyn stating I called to late, which was 5min after appt time. An appt time that was an hr before my court time. Which she appeared to have no issue in attending.
That is when I decided to end all work with the firm paid my balance in full and seeked council from Ms. Roberts at her new firm. Begining of Feb 2024 I bagan working with Ms. Roberts and it is confirmed that my divorce will be finalized by end of March 2024.

From my experience this firm appears to be profit oriented than client oriented. They appeared to seem to want to prolong my case with unnecessary stuff to accrue a higher balance. When I questiined their ethics and professionalism I was treated as if a Latina Women wouldn't have any knowledge. Trying to justify the HIPAA violation and submission of unnecessary petiton to the courts. Please be cautious with their services.

reviews.text:
In May, I hired Diana to help me understand a severance agreement from my employer. While Diana’s knowledge and interpretation of the agreement was sound, there are two areas that were less than satisfactory.

1. I also hired Diana to write a counter offer to the severance. The letter read a bit strong to me but I trusted her judgment. I later received feedback that the counter letter was so overly aggressive in tone that the employer closed the door on any opportunity for a counter.

2. After we had concluded our business, I received an unsolicited email from Diana just checking in and seeing how things resolved. The situation with my employer was a rough one so I thought she was sending a decent email to see how it wrapped up. So i respond to fill her in. We wound up having a couple of exchanges that in no way aided me at the time. And then nothing happens. 4 months later I receive an invoice for that email exchange.

I am disappointed In my experience with this firm.
Excellent service and professionalism. Friendly hard working staff.

Diana is what you want in an attorney.  She communicates plainly and in a timely manner.  She kept me informed and was always available to answer my questions.  She is thorough and realistic.  I do not like to have to hire attorneys.  With that said, Diana has changed how I feel about this process.
Diana and the team were great to work with. They were always available, patient with questions and helped me get a positive outcome.

This is the attorney you’re looking for. I had Diana Taylor represent me in a complicated defamation case, and found her to be more knowledgeable than other lawyers who had 30 years more experience than her. I have been represented by 2 other attorney firms in my lifetime, but Diana is the easiest for me to recommend to anyone. Even the judges involved in my case were impressed by Diana’s expertise, one claiming that she turned my “thread of a case into a silk purse.”

Beyond Diana’s rich knowledge of current law, what I most appreciated was her integrity. She made clear our boundaries of what we could and could not do from an ethical perspective, and I never felt like we were being dishonest in any way (which was personally important to me through this whole process).

I completely trust Diana’s expertise and ethics. If you need a reliable lawyer, please consider her law firm.

reviews.text:
I was involved in a serious car accident a few years ago, and I was referred to Michael through a mutual friend. Michael contacted me immediately to discuss my case. He listened carefully and made me feel comfortable from the very beginning.

At first, I thought that I did not need an attorney and that I could just represent myself, but after speaking with a few people, they suggested that I definitely get one.  I know that if I would have represented myself that I would have received enough money to pay my car off and maybe a little bit more for medical bills. Hiring Michael was definitely worth it.   After a few months of physical therapy and surgery, Michael took care of all the communication between the insurance company and myself. I trusted him and told him that if he thought the offer that they had made was fair that I would let him make the decision to accept it or not. He went back and forth for another week or two, and he was able to obtain a bit more.  Within a few weeks, I had a check in my hand.  He actually has helped me more than once.

If you're looking for an attorney who will listen to you, be patient,  is knowledgeable of the law, decisive, confident of his abilities, and his ability to help you, then Michael is the Personal Injury attorney who can help you too.  Hands down, he is the most hardworking and perservering attorney that I have dealt with!

Thank you, Michael!

reviews.text: I had the unfortunate experience of working with Fiona McCormick. My experience with her as my attorney was literally worse than I could have imagined.
