Secondly, the moment I was confused and ask questions about the documents and what my options were if I changed my mind she withdrew from my case THE DAY BEFORE I was to appear in court for the first time. She showed up to court just to quit and told the judge she couldnt work with me. She and the opposing attorney were literally scolded by the judge because it was obvious neither of them had worked our our case at all and showed up to court completely unprepared. The mediation document presented to the court was even wrong or not signed properly. After all this and quitting on me, she sent me an email for another bill for alleged "work" she had already done. Threatened file a motion to make me pay. So I ended up paying this attorney like 4-5k just to quit on me and do nothing else. My new attorney expressed some confusion at the shoddy work she had done and had to correct things and got a deal done for me after just a couple of court dates with far more reasonable terms by actually doing his job and working with the other attorney. I recommend staying away from this firm and Ms. McCormick. My experience working with them is they have very low morals and ethics and will quit on you if you have any questions or dare to change your mind about anything.

When I was planning my divorce, I didn't know how to go about finding someone to represent me.  I had received information about Del Re Law Group from my work. I thought it was too good to be true.

I reached out to another firm that was "supportive of women", and they were constantly playing phone tag to set up the consult. When it came time for the consult, they did NOT read my intake form nor ask me any questions about my experience. They also wanted a higher fee!

I then went back to my first option of Kat, and could not have been more happy. Also, the parent has NO choice but to agree to the GAL’s recommendation.  Because they are all in cahoots. So much for the law!
reviews.ownerResponse: I know the case this person is referring to and the name the person used to post this review is not the actual name of the party whose child was represented by our office.  With that being said, being appointed the Guardian ad Litem for children in custody cases is a very difficult job and you must represent the best interest of the child which means making tough decisions for the best of the child.  Many times, parties do not agree with the Guardian ad Litem's recommendations and lash out when they are unhappy by posting negative reviews, but the reality is, in this case, my work (Kat Allen) as Guardian ad Litem was reasoned and sound and the Court did agree with my recommendations based upon my investigation, even if this party was not happy with the outcome.   I certainly do not like making parties unhappy, but my utmost concern and responsibility in Guardian ad Litem cases is protecting the child and making recommendations that are in his or her best interest based upon the evidence that is provided to me.  Unfortunately, that sometimes leads to unhappy parties and unwarranted negative reviews, but that is just part of the job for a Guardian ad Litem.

reviews.ownerResponse: To clarify, this person did not hire our firm, Ms. Allen was appointed as the child's Guardian ad Litem by the Court. reviews.text:
My mother suffered a fall at her long-term care facility and subsequently death.  My family needed answers.  Professional referrals and careful research lead me to Salvi, Schostok & Pritchard, P. C..  Attorney John A. Mennie responded to my call.  He listened with compassion and respect, skillfully extracting the facts that were required for the initial investigation.   From the first meeting Mr. Mennie began to establish trust and demonstrated patience along with great listening and communication skills.  He was extremely responsive throughout the process, keeping me informed as the case progressed.

Mr. Mennie’s skill and knowledge in the personal injury and wrongful death space is exceptional.   I must also acknowledge and commend Attorney Jaclyn J. Kurth, who's skill and knowledge is equally exceptional.  I was especially impressed with the arguments and the evidence presented by this dynamic duo, which answered many questions and brought the closure that my family needed.

The life of my mother is priceless.  Our loss is immeasurable.   That said, I truly appreciate the hard work and perseverance of Mr. Mennie and Ms. Kurth which resulted in one of the highest settlements among its class in Lake County IL.

If you have similar concerns about a loved one’s care, I highly recommend Attorney John A. Mennie and/or Attorney Jaclyn J. Kurth at Salvi, Schostok & Pritchard P. C..

Sincerely,
Ms. White

reviews.text:
I worked with Dan and overall I would say my experience was unpleasant. He's a really polite guy but I feel pretty robbed considering I had to do half of his job for him. I got tricked into using him as my real estate attorney as well as probate, that was a mistake... I've made several really estate transactions in the past and it was frustrating to have literally 0 communication from Dan the ENTIRE time. In fact, I had to reach out to him about all the closing documents I never received from him (this was 3 days before closing and his office is over an hour away, closing date never changed either). He made me overnight all the documents back to him on my dime even though he dropped the ball.
As for probate, this is my first experience with probate so it's hard for me to say if this experience was acceptable or not. I do know that this was a VERY simple probate case, not sure it could have been any easier to handle from an administrative aspect.
He barely answered any of my questions, he would simply tell me to ask someone else (such as vehicle titles belonging to the deceased and tax return owed to the deceased). His dates on when the estate would be discharged were all messed up - again, I had to reach out and correct him and was given a run-around as to "why" he was almost a month off on his dates. And sure enough, it got discharged exactly when I said it would as opposed to his "guess".
Overall I wish I would have looked into a better attorney. I got more answers from my own Googling than I did from my attorney that I paid thousands of dollars for. I had realized my mistake in picking Dan early on but was unfortunately didn't really have an option of getting out of the check he had cashed, especially since he had rendered services to an extent. Really nice guy but strongly recommend using a different attorney, perhaps one more experienced?
reviews.ownerResponse:
I always do my best to have every client be satisfied with the work that I perform for them. Unfortunately, in this case in the end Ms. Kramer has a complaint of how the work that I performed was done. Even worse I find out about it through social media not from her directly. As I understand it there is no complaint that the probate estate closed in record time (six months and one week)(The actual closing of an Estate is determined by the Court and therefore cannot be exactly predicted as Ms. Kramer believed). The shortest possible time is six months. The real estate transaction closed in two weeks which is unheard of when most real estate transactions take between 30 to 60 days. (Overnight mail was a necessity to pull this closing off since it was such a short time frame). It had nothing to do with my dropping the ball or making a mistake as Ms. Kramer states.
First to address what Ms. Kramer believes to be my inexperience. It is true that I am a polite, 27 year old attorney but not an inexperienced one. I have been involved in various aspects of more than 40 different probate estates in four different counties. Additionally, I have closed more than 60 real estate transactions. As Ms. Kramer indicates she has never gone through probate before but yet she feels confident to draw a number of conclusions of how she thinks probate should have been completed. For instance, my referring her to an accountant or completing a transaction at the DMV was interpreted as me having her do my job. I am not an accountant and Ms. Kramer needed to participate to make the transfer of the vehicle at the DMV. I did my best to answer questions and provide explanations both in writing and verbally.
A few statements made by Ms. Kramer are just not true. She states I had "0" communication with her. This is not true I communicated both in writing and verbally.
I am not sure how I tricked Ms. Kramer into representing her in the real estate transaction. I was given the real estate contract and did the legal work necessary to close the transaction for the Estate. There was nothing tricky about that.
I assume the statement that she felt "robbed" refers to how much work Ms. Kramer felt she had to do and not me. When you are appointed as the Executor of an estate you have certain responsibilities that the attorney does not and cannot perform. It would seem difficult for Ms. Kramer to draw any of these conclusions since as she said she has never done this before. My hope is to educate a client during the process. That apparently was not successful in this case.
My business is based on the service that I provide. When a person provides a review that is wholly inaccurate I absolutely must set the record straight. Future clients will look at this to see if I am a competent attorney. It is unfortunate that these statement can be made without any accountability. Regardless of the misstatements that Ms. Kramer has made I do not hold any ill will toward her and wish her well.

reviews.text: I contacted Dan Hood, whom I had used previously for some simple estate settlement work. I told him that a dog breeder had breached our verbal agreement for a puppy and refused to refund my money. He charged me $300 to send her a letter and make a few phone calls to her, which did not succeed in getting a refund. Then he told me to forget about it, advising that I would lose the case if I sued her. He basically took her side and was demeaning to me for wanting to continue pursuing a refund. I filed a small claims case against her on my own and won. I got my full refund, plus all legal and filing fees back. Apparently Mr. Hood is unaware that when a business breaches their contract and is sued, they will be awarded their full refund plus legal fees. I think he just didn't want to be bothered with it. Maybe he is better at form letters and filling out basic paperwork to file in court than actually advocating for a paying client.

reviews.text: We talked on the phone, he said he would take my case for free as pro bona lawer. All he needed to do is write a single email. I drove 35 miles to come to the office and he tells me that “nothing is free and we need to pay for electricity”. He asked couple thosands and I didnt like it. At least they could tell it to me in advance.
reviews.ownerResponse:
The only true statement made by this gentleman is that we spoke on the telephone. Every other statement is false. He made an appointment to see me and canceled twice. He appeared today unannounced and told me about his case and requested that I represent him and send an e-mail on his behalf. I told him that I would represent him but that he had to pay me for the representation. Never did we discuss pro bono work on the telephone or at any other time. My working for free only came up today when he asked that I send an e-mail for him. We never discussed a fee since he wanted the work done for free. I did not quote a fee of thousands of dollars nor did I justify my request for a fee by saying I had to pay for my electricity.

Our initial consultation is always free but work beyond the initial consultation is a fee that the client and I discuss before any work is performed and mutually agreed upon. We never discussed a fee (certainly not thousands of dollars) since he demanded that I send an e-mail for free and that maybe he would give me additional business in the future if I did this for him. I refused and he stormed out of my office saying that by refusing to do what he asked would be bad for my business.

It's not a well-run firm:
- They took my information, then proceeded to mix up my phone number twice. Apparently leaving messages at some other number for my case.
- When I hired them, I could not get them to call me or email me to confirm they would appear on my behalf in court. The DAY OF, the senior attorney called me just minutes before court.
- 2nd court date, junior attorney wasn't prepared with the required documentation, so we were denied a reasonable request.
- Their agreement says that clients agree to them splitting up tasks, to be more cost-efficient. However, in reality, it means clients get charged many times for a task that could have been done by a single competent attorney in one session. For example, when I asked them to withdraw, because I was not seeing the value, they managed to rack up over $2,000 in further expenses just to withdraw: Junior attorney read email, sent to paralegal. Paralegal reviewed it. Junior attorney made revisions. Senior attorney reviewed it again. Revised again, Submitted. 1.5 hour court time talking about it.
- When I saw the final bill and disputed it in an itemized way as required, I got no response except to keep requesting payment over and over again.
- In short, they racked up $7k worth of expenses in 2 months with mostly junior level administrative tasks.
- Speaking of billing, this is a pain point too. One person asks for more money. I paid. Several days later, another person "reminded" me that I need to pay. I said I already paid. "Oh, we're sorry."  Then they did it several more times.

My advice is to find a single attorney or a firm with a flat rate by case type. I explored a flat rate attorney that was well-reviewed and they quoted $6k. I'm now in over $10k with this firm and didn't even get good representation. I'm now pro se (representing myself) and doing just as well.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hey Jeremy, thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re very sorry to hear that your experience with our firm left you feeling frustrated and disappointed. Clear communication, transparent billing, and effective representation are standards we strive to uphold, and it’s clear from your review that we fell short in several areas.

We take concerns like yours seriously and would appreciate the opportunity to review your case internally to understand what went wrong. If you're open to discussing this further, please reach out to me directly at Ryan@oflaherty-law.com. We’d like to take this chance to learn from your experience and see if there’s anything we can do to help resolve the issues you’ve raised.

reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Jensen, We are so very sorry this was the experience of the individual for whom you are a caregiver. It may be best to call the local office with the applicant for services and ask to speak to the managing attorney. You may state that you have a complaint about the services provided. reviews.text: I can't get ahold of prairie State Legal. I am on Section 8. Every time I call Prairie State Legal, all I get is a recording, that you have to push a lot of numbers, and you still can't talk to a lawyer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Renters seeking legal help related to rental housing should call 855-631-0811 (Illinois Eviction Help). You will be asked to enter your zip code and will be routed to available intake staff if you call Monday through Friday from 9am to 3pm.  Older Adults (60 or older) may call our Older Adults Legal Assistance Helpline 888-965-7757 Monday through Thursday between 9am and 1pm.  All other callers should call their local office which can be found at www.pslegal.org between 9am and 1pm Monday through Thursday. The demand for legal services exceeds the organization's capacity. For that reason priority is given to persons fleeing domestic violence and abuse, persons facing eviction or the loss or denials of government benefits such as SNAP.  It can be difficult for persons with important but less urgent issues to get through. We also offer an online system to start an application and that is on our website.  If the system routes the user to legal information it means we cannot provide legal services at that time. Other users of the online system will be given a special phone number that bypasses all of those prompts in the telephone intake system. Applicants still need to call that number between 9am and 1pm Monday through Thursday.  We are working on simplifying our intake system because we know it is frustrating. That may take some time but please know we are working on it.
Thank you for providing the positive rating.

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to let us know about your experience. When people apply for legal services our telephone intake attorneys gather the information. There are some types of cases that the local office attorneys typically handle such as domestic violence, evictions, denials or terminations of needs based benefits and utility shut offs and the case is transferred to the office for many of those cases. In cases that can be resolved with legal advice or which don't impact important issues, the intake attorneys provide legal advice and close the case. In unusual situations in which legal representation is needed, the telephone intake attorney will contact the local office to ask whether staff or volunteer attorneys are available to help. This is what probably happened in your situation. We recognized it was an important matter so the intake attorney tried to find out if any attorneys might be available. When we find out that we do not have resources to provide representation we attempt to tell the applicant as soon as we can. We are very sorry that you felt you were treated improperly.

reviews.text:
If you need legal representation I highly recommended Bur-Man Law Group.
Based on a trusted referral and reviewing their website I decided to call. My situation was multifaceted, however in the consultation I knew I need not look any further. The partners, attorney LaTonya Burton and attorney Renea   Amen, are intelligent, professional and savvy. They were patient and compassionate when I was emotional, they were thorough with their questions to understand the full scope of my situation and within minutes began citing, referencing and confirming case law that would apply to my situation.
They were honest and transparent in advising they may not be best suited to represent me considering the totality of what was ahead, but identified what they could take on if I wanted them to, (which I did). They were thoughtful enough to refer me to other legal counsel whose expertise would best serve me for other aspects of what I needed to pursue. They were intentional in gathering necessary evidence from me to support the case. They were fair with their fees. They were resourceful to ensure all bases were covered to position us for a favorable outcome and to support the next phase in my legal battle where other counsel would represent me. Their staff are also professional and responsive.
The attorneys at Bur-Man Law Group are experts in their field, creative in their approach and relentless in the courtroom. During my four-month ordeal I witnessed them being confronted with opposition and bias, which they faced steadfast and with poise. The ruling was in my/our favor, thanks to the partners and team at Bur-Man Law Group. If you need legal representation I highly recommend calling Bur-Man Law Group.

reviews.text: This is by far one of the best and most responsive legal teams in the nation when dealing with life altering situations. If you want prompt, responsive, and a caring legal team, by far they epitomize all of those qualities. I had a very tricky situation involving a gun case that I dealt with for three years. I dealt with the anxiety and worry involving my future. I dealt with another lawyer for the first year that by google terms was very highly rated. I would never recommend him to anybody, because of the lack of attentiveness. This stage in his journey, his only ambition is in dealing with “major cases“ . Upon firing him and working with Burman, Law Group, I not only got the respect I desired, but the focus and care as well from this team. Needless to say, they not only got the desired outcome, but held my hand through this entire journey. They are not only relentless in the court room. But they are honest about the capability of getting a desired outcome. If you are reading this comment, and have never been in a situation like this before I can tell you this. The number one thing you want outside of a win, is to feel like your legal team cares about you and is going to fight for you the way that you would fight for your family. I am forever, indebted and love this team like the older sisters, I never had.

Mark Curran provided some of the worst legal representation I have ever experienced. He put no meaningful effort into my case — he forgot court dates, failed to respond to my concerns, and ultimately blamed me for problems caused by an abusive family member.

He routinely could not remember basic details of my case and neglected to review paperwork he was required to read. When a deal was reached, he emailed me documents to sign — except they were the wrong documents entirely, and he never bothered to verify them before sending.

I had submitted records for him to review and had to follow up with him multiple times asking whether he had received them. Each time he said no. After weeks of this, he finally admitted he had not been to his office to check his mail because he was focused on other cases and campaigning for public office. I am not exaggerating.

I have left reviews of Mr. Curran in the past, and he has retained legal counsel to have them removed. I would treat any positive reviews of his practice with significant skepticism, as he has a pattern of suppressing critical feedback to manage his online reputation.

So I used their chat feature two days in a row and was told someone would get back to me..

Didn't happen.

Then I called on Friday and left a message just asking for you know courtesy to tell me whether or not they would even consider talking to me...

Never heard back.

Could be the greatest attorney in the world, however my opinion based on the lack of simple common courtesy is get somebody else because clearly a person whose job is to effectively communicate fails miserably.

And if you are the lucky one that gets to hire him? Yeah don't expect a whole lot of communication. This is what they're paid for.

It is the most simple and basic decent thing to do as a human being.

Don't lie to potential clients it definitely don't ignore them.

But what do you expect from a lawyer?

I had a truly outstanding experience working with David and his team on the closing of my home. From start to finish, they were responsive, thorough, and incredibly professional. David took the time to explain every step of the process clearly and always made himself available to answer questions, no matter how big or small.

There were a few unexpected challenges along the way, but David handled everything with calm and confidence, which gave me real peace of mind. His attention to detail and proactive communication ensured that the entire transaction moved forward smoothly.

I’m so grateful for their support and would highly recommend David and his team to anyone in need of a trustworthy, knowledgeable, and efficient real estate attorney.

David and his team closed a real estate transaction during a crucial transition period for my family.  We can’t tell you what a relief it was to have all of our concerns addressed promptly and professionally. Between legal advising from David and finalizing all the necessary particulars with Brittany, we were confident in the expertise and reliability of our team! We don’t use that phrase lightly, as investors, this was the first time for us using Morris Law but it certainly won’t be the last! Thanks for all you guys did for us!

My closing was on a Tuesday at 3:00 p.m. I went to my bank a week prior and set up the ability to wire money.  On Monday I asked Morris Law to get a final bill and they said they would reach out to the seller. I asked again on Tuesday they were finally able to provide. I saw I owed more than I expected so I had to hurry and find enough money to put into my bank account so that the amount on the bill would be covered.  This made for a hectic day but I was able to find the funds.
On Tuesday at 3pm, Morris Law walked into the closing office and found me on the phone with my bank frantically trying to get the wire transfer done. The annoyed closing clerk gave me the information that I was supposed to have received and I was on the phone when my attorney came in and sat next to me.
He opens my file, obviously the first time he's seeing it and asks, "they didn't give you wiring instructions?" (he was speaking of his associates). I said no. He dismissed his mistake and started with the paperwork.  While on hold with my bank I also asked him about the discrepancy on the bill. He said that I was given the wrong number and that it was less than what was originally given to me. That meant that scrounging up the last desperate dollars that I needed was not necessary. He did not apologize, he just said that I was given the wrong information.
For two hours we sat there, Morris suggested twice that I call my bank again and twice they said that the wire has been initiated, it was going into the approval stage but that it could take up to 24 hours. Morris Law is sitting with me in this small room and he can hear all of this.  The closing clerk came in every 15 minutes reminding me that she still hasn't seen the money.  After two hours the office was closing and he let me know that delaying it a day was not ideal but that it should still work. This coming from the person who did not tell me to start the wire transfer, and who gave me the wrong total which was significantly different from the original bill I was given.  I have emails of all of this if you want to see them. He sauntered to his car while I freaked out in mine.
The next day! On Wednesday, I received two emails from his firm, one email from him, two phone calls from his firm, one phone call from him and two texts! I am being incessantly contacted by Morris. He asked when the money was coming. He said that money transfers do not take 24 hours. I am in disbelief because... HE WAS SITTING NEXT TO ME WHILE MY BANK  EXPLAINED TO US THAT IT COULD TAKE 24 HOURS. He was being so callous and asking me if I was checking my bank account and refreshing the app. Of course I was! I am purchasing a new house! He threatened that the title company would put extra interest on the seller's mortgage which would affect my bill. The audacity! The situation of the wire being delayed was because he failed to give me, his client, the appropriate instructions. His one and only job was to allow me to walk out of the closing on Tuesday with keys in my hand. He did not accomplish the only thing he was hired to do.  He told me that he paid out of pocket, for the $9.47 interest that the seller accrued on that day. Out of pocket!?! He owes me a spa day for how many emails, texts and phone calls I had to make. For the anxiety I had because perhaps I wasn't going to get the home that I was purchasing.
I finally receive the confirmation that the wire was successful and then I am given directions on where to pick up the keys. (If I were a lawyer that messed up this badly, I would have offered to drive them to my client).
It is obvious he did not even know my name before pulling into the parking lot that Tuesday. He had a folder full of different clients names. I was just another paycheck.  I was given the wrong dollar amount and no wiring instructions, my lawyer was tasked with this and failed.  I walked out of the closing without a house.
I would not recommend this firm to anybody. I found them to be greedy, incompetent and unapologetic. I regret ever calling them.
Always AVAILABLE!! Answers my questions

reviews.text: I hired this law firm to represent me in my divorce case. Since the beginning of working with this attorney, I noticed that Jean my attorney would not respond to my emails nor would she send me the documentation that I requested from her. Often times she would call me the same day we had court to “loop me in” instead of speaking to be prior to the court date. Many times she asked to make decisions about my case and give me 30 minutes to decide. I would bring many issue about my case to her attention and she would not take action. She would wait last minute to submit motions that would have helped my case if she would have done that from the beginning.She would say I didn’t email her required documents for my case( when I did) and she would always forget information about my case. I have two children so it turn into a custody battle. My attorney would let the opposing party take the win while we took the loses. I guess Jean was fine with billing me for all this “work” even if that “work” didn’t amount to anything.I would not recommend this firm to anyone

He was also quick to respond to phone calls & emails. They negotiated a settlement that was more than satisfactory to me.  They were also instrumental in putting me in touch with someone who helped me decide how to best invest some of the funds to my benefit.  I honestly don't know what I would have done without this firm.  I am a single woman who had no experience with a personal injury case before. I cannot recommend them enough.

I just wanted to share my experience with Gibson & Steigauf and his incredible Paralegal Staff.
After a nursing home for my father turned my dads life and mine (his son) upside down I needed a good atty. With that I went to a long time family atty and he recommended Gibson & Steigauf's office and boy am I glad he did.
The nursing home had flipped my dad's daily medicine to his roomates medicine. Results were he was being given daily doses of diabetic medication and he has never been near a diabetic. Results were 19 days in the hospital and in a diabetic coma. My dad did recover and moved on to the best he could.
I was so mad while being so busy at work Scott and his staff took over full steam ahead. Every time I talked to him or his staff I was totally confident that I was in good hands and can continue once again with my work, my dad and my personal life. Their memory to detail is nothing short of razor sharp and our settlement which I never put a number in my head was more than I ever considered. Please, if you read this review I'm telling you that you will not be sorry with your results. Let their office take the ball and run with it. One can actually move on with their life during and after the process. I thank you and your office Mr. Scott Gibson!

reviews.text:
My wife and I contacted John Steigauf after being hit by drunk driver.  We had zero liability in the accident as we were stopped at a red light and rear ended by the drunk driver at 45mph.  We had to be extracted from our car by the fire department and transported to the hospital with neck and lower back injuries.  Despite clear liability by the drunk driver and injuries that occurred as a result of the accident both insurance companies stalled the claims process.

From the beginning, John clearly explained to us the onerous and lengthy process of dealing with the insurance companies.  He explained how the insurance companies work, their attempts to avoid liability, and what we would need to do ensure that the companies are held accountable.  John continuously updated us and walked us through the process with patience and a genuine concern for us.  John has a keen sense of justice for his clients and tenaciously goes after the insurance companies when he sees them trying to take advantage of his clients.   John's guidance and determination in getting us justice was critical in coming to a settlement with the insurance companies.

I highly recommend John as a caring and effective lawyer.
